- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). <observation>
  <type>decision</type>
  <title>FeatureWheel Trail Effect Implementation Plan</title>
  <subtitle>Planning dual-spine trail system for FeatureWheel with pooling and dynamic skin/bone positioning</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FlyManager.ts will receive new FLY_EVENT for Trail effect implementation</fact>
    <fact>Two trail spines required: EyeStrike2_Function_Trail_L for wheels 1-2-3, EyeStrike2_Function_Trail_R for wheels 4-5-6</fact>
    <fact>Each spine has single animation "In" and four skins: default, Multiplier, Shift, Upgrade</fact>
    <fact>Trail spines will use object pooling pattern</fact>
    <fact>Trail_End_Control bone position must be transformed to target location before animation playback</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>A new trail effect system is being planned for FeatureWheel functionality. The implementation will add a FLY_EVENT handler in FlyManager.ts that manages two separate Spine trail objects - one for the left side (wheels 1-2-3) and one for the right side (wheels 4-5-6). The system requires properties to configure which spine asset to use. Each trail spine contains a single "In" animation and supports four different visual skins (default, Multiplier, Shift, Upgrade) that can be switched dynamically. The implementation will follow existing FlyManager patterns: spawn the spine from a pool at the starting position, transform the Trail_End_Control bone to the target endpoint, select the appropriate skin, then trigger the "In" animation. This approach maintains consistency with existing fly effect implementations while adding the visual trail capability.</narrative>

   - Added proper parameters: roundIndex, start/end points, skin type via TypeToSkin, and callback
   - TypeScript compilation passed (no errors except existing deprecation warnings)

   - The trail system uses left spine (EyeStrike2_Function_Trail_L) for rounds 0-2 and right spine (EyeStrike2_Function_Trail_R) for rounds 3-5
   - Skin selection is determined by Type via TypeToSkin (Multiplier/Upgrade/Shift, or default)
   - Start point comes from FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.GET_TARGET_NODE
   - End points are world coordinates of landing positions
   - Uses Promise-dispatch pattern with callbacks

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Position Coordinate System and Conversion Functions</title>
  <subtitle>Codebase uses col/row pairs and encoded server positions with multiple conversion utilities</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Server positions encoded as col*4+row format, decoded via Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig</fact>
    <fact>SlotReels.GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromColRow converts col/row to world coordinates</fact>
    <fact>SlotReels.GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition converts encoded position to world coordinates</fact>
    <fact>SlotReels.GetPositionFromColRow validates ranges and converts col/row to single position number</fact>
    <fact>FeatureWheelShowState.ts line 265 already uses GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition for endpoint calculation</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The codebase implements a dual coordinate system for slot positions. Server data encodes positions as col*4+row (single integer), which is decoded via Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig into {col, row} objects. The SlotReels class provides multiple conversion functions: GetPositionFromColRow validates column/row ranges and converts to single position numbers, GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromColRow/GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition both return world coordinates (Vec3) for visual placement. These functions are used extensively across feature systems (FeatureWheel, Multiplier, Respin, Scatter) to locate symbols on the reel grid. The position functions include validation that errors on out-of-range inputs. For the trail implementation, FeatureWheelShowState already uses GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition at line 265 to calculate endpoint world coordinates, matching the established pattern used by other fly effect systems.</narrative>

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Server Position Encoding Uses Variable Row Heights</title>
  <subtitle>DecodeServerPosByRowConfig uses Game_Define.ROW_CONFIG for column-specific heights, not fixed col*4+row formula</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig iterates through Game_Define.ROW_CONFIG to decode positions</fact>
    <fact>Each column can have different heights defined in Game_Define.ROW_CONFIG array</fact>
    <fact>Position encoding accumulates heights across columns rather than using fixed multiplication</fact>
    <fact>Common.EncodeServerPosByRowConfig validates row against column-specific height before encoding</fact>
    <fact>Functions return null/-1 for out-of-range inputs rather than throwing errors</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The server position encoding system is more sophisticated than a simple col*4+row formula. Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig uses Game_Define.ROW_CONFIG to handle variable-height columns, iterating through columns and accumulating their individual heights to find which column contains the encoded position. This allows the game to support irregular reel configurations where different columns have different numbers of visible symbols. The corresponding EncodeServerPosByRowConfig validates that the row index is within the specific column's height before encoding. Both functions use graceful error handling (null/-1 returns) rather than throwing exceptions, allowing calling code to handle invalid positions without try-catch blocks.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

content.thinking:
The user is reading FeatureResultShowState.ts to see how it handles position decoding and validation. This shows:
1. Using DecodeServerPosByRowConfig to decode server positions
2. Checking if decoded is null before using it
3. Using the decoded col/row for validation (isShownCash)
4. Building upgrade data by filtering and collecting positions

This is discovery of existing patterns for handling server positions in the feature system.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Feature Position Validation Pattern</title>
  <subtitle>FeatureResultShowState validates decoded positions and filters by game state before processing upgrades</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FeatureResultShowState.ts decodes upgrade positions via Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig</fact>
    <fact>Code checks if decoded result is null before accessing col/row properties</fact>
    <fact>isShownCash helper validates if symbol at decoded col/row is appropriate for upgrade</fact>
    <fact>upgradePositionSet tracks processed positions to prevent duplicates between round results and persistent markers</fact>
    <fact>Persistent wheel features from previous rounds are merged with current round upgrade results</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>FeatureResultShowState demonstrates the standard pattern for processing feature positions. It decodes server positions using DecodeServerPosByRowConfig, null-checks the result, then validates the decoded col/row against game state criteria (e.g., isShownCash ensures only Cash symbols get upgraded). The code maintains a Set to track already-processed positions, preventing duplicates when merging current round upgrade results with persistent wheel features carried over from previous rounds. This defensive pattern (decode → null-check → game-state-validation → dedup) ensures robust handling of server data while supporting the game's persistent feature mechanics where wheel effects can span multiple rounds.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>assets/Script/GameState/FeatureResultShowState.ts</file>
  </files_read>

  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
This observation shows the implementation of GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition function in SlotReels.ts. This is relevant to the trail effect implementation as it's the function being used to convert positions to world coordinates for the trail endpoints.

Key findings:
- GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition takes a one-dimensional position index
- It retrieves an empty node via GetMiddleSymbolEmptyNodeFromPosition
- Returns world position Vec3, or v3(0,0,0) if invalid
- Includes error logging for invalid nodes

This is a discovery about the utility function being used in the trail implementation.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>SlotReels Position-to-World Coordinate Conversion</title>
  <subtitle>GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition converts one-dimensional position index to world Vec3 with fallback handling</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SlotReels.GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition accepts one-dimensional position index (0 to N-1)</fact>
    <fact>Function retrieves empty node via GetMiddleSymbolEmptyNodeFromPosition internally</fact>
    <fact>Returns world position Vec3 by calling emptyNode.getWorldPosition()</fact>
    <fact>Invalid nodes log error and return v3(0,0,0) as safe fallback</fact>
    <fact>Function validates node existence and isValid property before coordinate extraction</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>SlotReels.GetSymbolInitWorldPositionFromPosition is the key utility for converting encoded positions to visual coordinates. It abstracts the complexity of position-to-node mapping by accepting the one-dimensional server position format and internally resolving it to the appropriate middle symbol empty node. The function extracts world coordinates via Cocos Creator's getWorldPosition() API, which accounts for the node hierarchy's combined transforms. Error handling is defensive rather than throwing—invalid positions log an error but return a zero vector, preventing crashes while making issues visible in logs. This design allows calling code to remain simple while the function handles edge cases internally. The trail implementation relies on this function to calculate endpoint world coordinates for the spine animation's Trail_End_Control bone transformation.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

FEATURE_TRAIL_ANIM_NAME: string = \\\"In\\\";\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch FeatureWheel Trail 預設 Skin（非法 skinName fallback 用）\\n     */\\n    private static readonly FEATURE_TRAIL_DEFAULT_SKIN: string = \\\"default\\\";\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch FeatureWheel Trail 輪次 L/R 切點（roundIndex < 此值用左，否則用右）\\n     */\\n    private static readonly FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT: number = 3;\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 需要註冊的事件列表\\n     */\\n    private static readonly EVENTS_TO_REGISTER = [\\n        FLY_EVENT.PLAY_FLY_TO_COLLECT_ANIMATION,\\n        FLY_EVENT.PLAY_ADD_CASH_ANIMATION,\\n        FLY_EVENT.PLAY_SHOW_SCATTER_TO_MAGIC_POT_ANIMATION,\\n        FLY_EVENT.CANCEL_ADD_CASH_ANIMATION,\\n        FLY_EVENT.PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION,\\n    ];\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 是否註冊事件\\n     */\\n    private m_isEventRegistered: boolean = false;\\n\\n    /**@ch PlayAddCashAnimation 取消令牌，每次呼叫遞增，舊的排程檢測到不一致時提前退出 */\\n    private m_addCashCancelId: number = 0;\\n    //#endregion 變數\\n\\n    //#region 方法\\n    onLoad() {\\n        this.RegisterEvent();\\n\\n        if ( this.m_flyToCollectPrefab )\\n        {\\n            PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().RegisterPrefab( CashType.Cash.toString(), this.m_flyToCollectPrefab, FlyManager.FLY_TO_COLLECT_POOL_SIZE );\\n        }\\n        if ( this.m_addCashSpinePrefab )\\n        {\\n            PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().RegisterPrefab( FlyManager.ADD_CASH_POOL_KEY, this.m_addCashSpinePrefab, FlyManager.ADD_CASH_POOL_SIZE );\\n        }\\n        if ( this.m_scatterToMagicPotPrefab )\\n        {\\n            PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().RegisterPrefab( FlyManager.SCATTER_TO_MAGIC_POT_POOL_KEY, this.m_scatterToMagicPotPrefab, FlyManager.SCATTER_TO_MAGIC_POT_POOL_SIZE );\\n        }\\n        if ( this.m_featureTrailPrefabL )\\n        {\\n            PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().RegisterPrefab( FlyManager.FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_L, this.m_featureTrailPrefabL, FlyManager.FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_SIZE );\\n        }\\n        if ( this.m_featureTrailPrefabR )\\n        {\\n            PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().RegisterPrefab( FlyManager.FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_R, this.m_featureTrailPrefabR, FlyManager.FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_SIZE );\\n        }\\n\\n        this.RegisterTestKeyCodes();\\n    }\\n\\n    onDestroy() {\\n        this.UnregisterEvent();\\n        this.UnregisterTestKeyCodes();\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 註冊事件\\n     */\\n    private RegisterEvent() {\\n        if (this.m_isEventRegistered) return;\\n\\n * @ch 取消註冊事件\\n     */\\n    private UnregisterEvent() {\\n        if (!this.m_isEventRegistered) return;\\n\\n        FlyManager.EVENTS_TO_REGISTER.forEach(eventName => {\\n            eventManager.UnRegister(eventName, this);\\n        });\\n\\n        this.m_isEventRegistered = false;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 事件處理\\n     * @param event 事件名稱\\n     * @param args 參數\\n content.thinking:
The user is editing FlyManager.ts to change the parameter name from `roundIndex` to `colIndex` in the JSDoc comment for the PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION event. This is a documentation update that clarifies that the parameter represents the column index of the endpoint on the playfield (0-5), rather than the round index. The logic remains the same (0-2 uses left trail, 3-5 uses right trail), but the semantic meaning is clearer - it's based on which column the trail is targeting, not which round it is.

This is a change-type observation focusing on documentation clarity.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Trail Event Parameter Renamed from roundIndex to colIndex</title>
  <subtitle>Documentation updated to clarify trail selection based on target column rather than round number</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION parameter renamed from roundIndex to colIndex in JSDoc</fact>
    <fact>Parameter represents target column index (0-5) on the playfield for endpoint positioning</fact>
    <fact>Logic unchanged: columns 0-2 use left Trail_L, columns 3-5 use right Trail_R</fact>
    <fact>Semantic clarification aligns parameter name with its actual usage as spatial column indicator</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>FlyManager.ts documentation updated to rename the trail animation event parameter from roundIndex to colIndex. This clarifies that trail selection (left vs right) is determined by the target column index on the playfield rather than the round number in the wheel spin sequence. While the numeric ranges remain identical (0-2 for left, 3-5 for right), the new name better represents the parameter's role as a spatial position indicator rather than a temporal round counter. This change improves code readability by making it clear that trail routing is based on where the effect lands on the grid, which happens to align with the wheel round progression but is conceptually about the destination column.</narrative>

    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>

    <file>assets/Script/FlyManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Updated Trail Split Constant Documentation</title>
  <subtitle>FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT constant documentation clarified to reference column index instead of round index</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT constant comment updated from roundIndex to colIndex terminology</fact>
    <fact>Documentation now explicitly states COL=6 configuration: columns 0-2 use left, 3-5 use right</fact>
    <fact>Constant value remains 3, representing the split point between left and right trails</fact>
    <fact>Change aligns documentation with spatial column-based semantics rather than temporal round-based semantics</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT constant documentation was updated to align with the earlier parameter rename from roundIndex to colIndex. The new comment explicitly clarifies that this constant represents a playfield column split point rather than a round number, and adds concrete detail that with COL=6 (6 columns total), columns 0-2 use the left trail and columns 3-5 use the right trail. This documentation improvement makes the constant's purpose clearer: it divides the grid spatially rather than temporally, even though the wheel round progression happens to match the spatial column layout.</narrative>

同時」對稱。兩者作用對象不同（GameView transform vs BigWheel Spine），\\n            // 無共用 target，可安全並行。\\n            await Promise.all( [ this.StopBoardEffect(), this.PlayWheelExit() ] );\\n        }\\n        finally\\n        {\\n            // 安全網：例外路徑保證盤面還原（happy path 已並行還原）。\\n            // RestoreZoom 冪等——m_isZoomed 在還原完成才設 false，故 happy path resolve 後此處 no-op，不會縮兩次。\\n            await this.StopBoardEffect();\\n        }\\n\\n        eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.RESUME_IDLE );\\n\\n        stateManager.NextState( Game_Define.GameState.ROUND_SHOW_END );\\n    }\\n\\n content.thinking:
This edit shows the implementation being updated to rename the parameter from `roundIndex` to `colIndex` in the actual function signature and body. The documentation (JSDoc), parameter name, inline comments, and error logging all now use `colIndex` instead of `roundIndex`. This completes the semantic rename from temporal (round number) to spatial (column index) terminology.

Key changes:
1. Function parameter renamed from `roundIndex` to `colIndex`
2. JSDoc updated to reflect colIndex
3. Inline comment changed from "依輪次選左/右 Trail" to "依終點盤面 column 選左/右 Trail"
4. Error log message updated to show `colIndex=${colIndex}` instead of `roundIndex=${roundIndex}`

This is the final piece of the rename refactoring that makes the code semantically clearer about what the parameter represents.

<observation>
  <type>refactor</type>
  <title>Completed roundIndex to colIndex Parameter Rename</title>
  <subtitle>Function signature and implementation fully updated from temporal round index to spatial column index</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation parameter renamed from roundIndex to colIndex in function signature</fact>
    <fact>Variable usage updated throughout function body from roundIndex to colIndex</fact>
    <fact>Inline comment changed from "依輪次選左/右 Trail" to "依終點盤面 column 選左/右 Trail"</fact>
    <fact>Error log message updated to display colIndex instead of roundIndex for diagnostics</fact>
    <fact>Logic unchanged: still compares against FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT (3) to select left or right trail</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The trail animation function refactoring is now complete. The parameter has been fully renamed from roundIndex to colIndex throughout the implementation - in the function signature, JSDoc documentation, inline comments, and error messages. This semantic shift clarifies that trail selection is based on the target column's spatial position on the playfield (0-5) rather than the temporal round number in the wheel spin sequence. The inline comment now reads "依終點盤面 column 選左/右 Trail" (select left/right trail based on endpoint column) instead of "依輪次選左/右 Trail" (select based on round). While the numeric values happen to align (rounds 1-3 land in columns 0-2, rounds 4-6 land in columns 3-5), the new naming makes it clear that the function operates on spatial grid coordinates, improving code clarity for future maintainers.</narrative>

    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>

content.thinking:
This is an edit to FeatureWheelShowState.ts that reverts the roundIndex-based loop back to a simpler for-of loop over features. The user had previously changed from `for (const wf of features)` to `for (let roundIndex = 0; roundIndex < features.length; roundIndex++)` in order to get the round index. Now they're reverting it back.

This suggests they realized they don't need roundIndex in this scope anymore, or they're going to calculate it differently. Looking at the earlier observations, they had been passing roundIndex to PlayFlyEffect, but now they need to determine the column index (colIndex) from the actual landing position data instead.

This is interesting - they're simplifying the loop because the colIndex should come from the actual position data (which column the effect lands on) rather than which round it is. This makes sense with the earlier semantic change from roundIndex to colIndex.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>refactor</type>
  <title>Reverted Feature Loop from Index-Based to For-Of</title>
  <subtitle>Simplified iteration back to for-of pattern, removing roundIndex variable</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FeatureWheelShowState.ts OnEnter loop changed from indexed for loop back to for-of loop</fact>
    <fact>roundIndex variable removed from loop scope</fact>
    <fact>Direct access to wf element restored via for-of iteration</fact>
    <fact>Loop body logic remains otherwise unchanged</fact>
    <fact>This reverses the earlier change that introduced roundIndex for trail selection</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The feature wheel iteration loop was simplified from an indexed for loop back to the original for-of pattern. The earlier refactor had introduced a roundIndex variable (0-based loop counter) to pass to PlayFlyEffect for trail selection. This revert suggests a design change: rather than determining trail selection from the temporal round number, it will be calculated from the actual landing position data (which column the effect lands on). This aligns with the broader semantic shift from roundIndex to colIndex throughout the trail system - the trail selection should be based on spatial position (where the effect lands on the grid) rather than temporal sequence (which round it is). The simpler for-of loop is more idiomatic when index calculation isn't needed at the loop level.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/GameState/FeatureWheelShowState.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
The user is continuing to observe changes from the primary session. This change removes the roundIndex parameter from the PlayFlyEffect call and updates the comment to indicate that trail selection will be based on each landing position's column rather than the round index.

This is a design change - instead of passing the round number (which was used to determine left/right trail), the implementation will now derive the column from the actual landing position data. This aligns with the overall refactor from temporal (round-based) to spatial (column-based) semantics.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>refactor</type>
  <title>Removed roundIndex Parameter from PlayFlyEffect Call</title>
  <subtitle>Trail selection will now derive column from position data rather than round number</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>PlayFlyEffect call no longer passes roundIndex parameter in OnEnter loop</fact>
    <fact>Comment updated from "roundIndex 決定左/右 Trail" to "每個落點依其盤面 column 決定左/右 Trail"</fact>
    <fact>Design shift: trail selection will be calculated from landing position column instead of passed as round index</fact>
    <fact>PlayFlyEffect function signature will need updating to match this change</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The call to PlayFlyEffect was updated to remove the roundIndex parameter, completing the shift from temporal to spatial trail selection. The updated comment clarifies the new design: "每個落點依其盤面 column 決定左/右 Trail" (each landing position determines left/right trail based on its playfield column). This means PlayFlyEffect will now need to derive the column index from the position data itself rather than receiving the round number as a parameter. This design is more robust because it bases trail selection on actual spatial data (where the effect lands) rather than relying on an assumption that round numbers correlate with columns. The change supports edge cases where the round-to-column mapping might not be perfectly sequential, making the system more data-driven and less assumption-dependent.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/GameState/FeatureWheelShowState.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

包一層 Promise 等退場 Out 播畢（PLAY_EXIT；Spine 缺失由 Manager 立即回調）\\n     */\\n    private PlayWheelExit(): Promise<void>\\n    {\\n        return new Promise<void>( resolve =>\\n        {\\n            eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.PLAY_EXIT, () => resolve() );\\n        } );\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 包一層 Promise 等單次 PLAY_SPIN 完成回調\\n     * @param landIndex 落點格索引（已由呼叫端確認 0~11 eventManager.Dispatch(\\n                FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.PLAY_SPIN,\\n                landIndex,\\n                ( result: SpinResult ) => resolve( result ?? null )\\n            );\\n        } );\\n    }\\n\\n    public OnProcess()\\n    {\\n\\n    }\\n\\n    public OnLeave()\\n    {\\n\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 播放飛行效果（FeatureWheel Trail：自輪盤中心飛向盤面落點，依功能 type 切 Skin）\\n     * 左/右 Trail 由「終點落在盤面哪一 column（0~5）」決定（col < 3 左、>= 3 右），逐落點各自判定。\\n     * @param data 飛行效果資料\\n     */\\n    private async PlayFlyEffect( data: ar2es2Proto.IFeatureResult )\\n    {\\n        log( content.thinking:
This is a big change! The PlayFlyEffect function has been completely refactored:

1. Removed the `roundIndex` parameter from the function signature
2. Updated JSDoc to explain that trail selection is now based on the endpoint column (0-5) with col < 3 for left, >= 3 for right
3. Added position decoding logic inside the map function using `Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig(pos)`
4. Added null-check for the decoded result with warning and skip if decoding fails
5. Changed the event dispatch to pass `decoded.col` instead of `roundIndex`

This is the key implementation change that makes the trail selection truly spatial (based on where the effect lands) rather than temporal (based on which round it is). Each position is independently decoded to get its column, and that column index is what determines left vs right trail.

This is much more robust because:
- It doesn't assume rounds map to columns in any particular way
- Each landing position can independently determine its trail
- Multiple positions in the same round but different columns will get appropriate trails
- The system is data-driven from the actual position coordinates

The defensive coding with the null-check and Promise.resolve() fallback ensures that if position decoding fails, it won't hang the Promise.all() - it will just skip that particular trail and warn.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Implemented Position-Based Trail Selection</title>
  <subtitle>PlayFlyEffect now decodes position to column for per-landing-point trail selection</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>PlayFlyEffect function signature updated to remove roundIndex parameter</fact>
    <fact>JSDoc updated to document trail selection based on endpoint column (col < 3 left, >= 3 right)</fact>
    <fact>Added Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig call inside position map to extract column from encoded position</fact>
    <fact>Null-check added for decode failure with warning and Promise.resolve() skip to prevent hang</fact>
    <fact>Event dispatch now passes decoded.col instead of roundIndex for trail selection</fact>
    <fact>Common import implicitly required (likely already imported from earlier discoveries)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>PlayFlyEffect was fully refactored to implement position-based trail selection. Instead of receiving a roundIndex parameter indicating which round is playing, the function now decodes each landing position to extract its column index (0-5) using Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig. This decoded column is passed to FlyManager for trail selection (columns 0-2 use left trail, 3-5 use right trail). The implementation includes defensive error handling: if position decoding fails, it logs a warning and returns Promise.resolve() to skip that trail without blocking the Promise.all() await. This design is more robust than round-based selection because it bases trail routing on actual spatial data (where the effect lands on the grid) rather than temporal sequence assumptions (which round it is). It supports edge cases where round-to-column mapping might not be perfectly sequential, and allows multiple positions in the same round but different columns to each get the appropriate trail independently.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/GameState/FeatureWheelShowState.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

第一次發動」；重轉（STEP6）仍為後續 plate。\\n *\\n * 職責邊界（scope = 骨架+轉輪演出）：\\n *   IN  — SHOW_UI → 依 WheelFeatures 逐項 PLAY_SPIN(SlotIndex) 序列等待 → HIDE_UI → ROUND_SHOW_END\\n *   OUT — 落點後的盤面演出（符號轉 CASH / 乘倍 badge / 升級 / FG 交棒）由後續 plate 停輪或後續階段處理\\n *\\n * 不在此 State 改動狀態機路由：FG（Type=0/4）的實際進場仍走既有 CheckState/LeaveRespin 的 IsGoingToFree。\\n */\\n@ccclass( \\\"FeatureWheelShowState\\\" )\\nexport class FeatureWheelShowState extends BaseState\\n{\\n    //#region 進場盤面效果參數（震動 + 盤面往下；需配合畫面實測微調）\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 盤面位移量（傳給 ZOOM_IN_REEL 的 targetLocalPos）。\\n     * ⚠️ ZoomInReel 公式：targetPos = 原座標 − targetLocalPos×scale → y 取「正」時盤面往「下」。\\n     *    動的是 GetZoomTarget()（GameView 整體），實際位移幅度需看畫面微調；BigWheel 若為其子節點會一起被移動。\\n     */\\n    private static readonly BOARD_ZOOM_POS = new Vec3( 0, 40, 0 );\\n\\n    /**@ch 盤面縮放（1 = 只位移不放大；>1 會放大整個 GameView）*/\\n    private static readonly BOARD_ZOOM_SCALE = 1.2;\\n\\n    /**@ch 盤面往下（ZoomIn）動畫時長（秒）*/\\n    private static readonly BOARD_ZOOM_IN_DURATION = 0.8;\\n\\n    /**@ch 盤面還原（ZoomOut）動畫時長（秒）*/\\n    private static readonly BOARD_ZOOM_OUT_DURATION = 0.2;\\n    //#endregion\\n    \\n    /**@ch 落點顯示停留時間（秒）*/\\n    private static readonly RESULT_STAY_DURATION = 1;\\n    \\n    /**@ch 停留時間（秒）*/\\n    private static readonly STAY_DURATION = 0.8;\\n\\n    private m_gameView: GameView = null;\\n\\n    constructor( gameView: GameView )\\n    {\\n        super();\\n        this.m_gameView = gameView;\\n    }\\n\\n    public async OnEnter()\\n    {\\n        log( \\\"OnEnter State : FEATURE_WHEEL_SHOW\\\" );\\n\\n        // 只取「有效」轉輪項（Type≠None）。理論上進到此 State 必有 ≥1（AfterShowWin 已用 HasActiveWheel 守衛），\\n        // 此處再過濾一次防禦：把混在其中的 None 項剔除，避免無意義轉動。\\n        const features = ( this.m_gameView.RoundInfo?.WheelFeatures ?? [] )\\n            .filter( w => w != null && w.Type !== FeatureWheelSegmentType.None );\\n\\n        if ( features.length === 0 )\\n        {\\n            // 不應發生（守衛已擋）；fail-loud 但不炸狀態機，直接續流程\\n            console.warn( \\\"[FeatureWheelShowState] 無有效 WheelFeatures，跳過轉輪演出\\\" );\\n            stateManager.NextState( Game_Define.GameState.ROUND_SHOW_END );\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n        // 進場盤面效果：震動只在進場「震一下就停」（單次自停，避免轉動時持續晃很怪）；盤面往下(ZoomIn)持續整段、退場才還原。\\n        // 用 try/finally 保證 StopBoardEffect 一定執行（ZoomIn 記原始狀態，未配對盤面回不去）。\\n        this.StartBoardEffect();\\n        try\\n        {\\n            // 進場 Active（盤面下降+灰塵+光效）：一個 round 一次，落在第一次轉動前；await 播畢（Spine 缺失則立即返回不 hang）\\n            await this.PlayWheelEnter();\\n\\n            // WheelCount 迴圈：逐項「序列等待」——PlaySpin 有 m_isSpinning 重入守衛，\\n            // 未等前一次 callback 就發下一次會被靜默丟棄（FeatureWheelManager 行為，BC-2 硬約束）。\\n            for ( const wf of features )\\n            {\\n                const landIndex = wf.SlotIndex ?? 0;\\n\\n                // 防 hang：PlaySpin 對越界 landIndex 是 warn+return（不呼叫 callback）→ 若 await 會永久掛住。\\n                // 故在 dispatch 前先驗範圍，越界則 fail-loud 跳過該次（不 await）。\\n                if ( landIndex < 0 || landIndex >= FEATURE_WHEEL_SEGMENT_COUNT )\\n                {\\n                    console.warn( `[FeatureWheelShowState] SlotIndex=${landIndex} 越界 0~${FEATURE_WHEEL_SEGMENT_COUNT - 1}，跳過該次轉動` );\\n                    continue;\\n                }\\n\\n                const result = await this.PlaySpinOnce( landIndex );\\n\\n                // fail-loud 一致性：落點 type 應與 server WheelFeatures.Type 相符\\n                // （前提 P：server SlotIndex 與客戶端 12 格序一致——待 server 對拍；不符僅 warn 不阻流程）\\n                if ( result != null && result.type !== wf.Type )\\n                {\\n                    console.warn( `[FeatureWheelShowState] 落點 type 不一致：landed=${result.type} vs server=${wf.Type}（SlotIndex=${landIndex}）` );\\n                }\\n                \\n                // 落點顯示停留時間\\n                await Define.Wait( this.m_gameView, FeatureWheelShowState.RESULT_STAY_DURATION );\\n\\n                // 飛個效果下來到盤面指定位置（每個落點依其盤面 column 決定左/右 Trail）\\n                await this.PlayFlyEffect( wf );\\n\\n                // ②標記 seam：通知演出端在盤面標記此功能（標記/icon/乘倍/Grand報獎/FreeGame盤外 由同事 handler 渲染）。\\n                await this.PlayMarkEffect( wf );\\n\\n                // 落點顯示已由 BigWheel Collect 動畫（PlaySpin 內 await）負責，中間維持亮起至退場 Out；\\n                // 故不再需要結果展示牌的暫代 dwell 與 HIDE_RESULT。\\n            }\\n\\n            // ③轉換 seam：標記後、進 respin 前，套用符號轉換（一般符號→CASH）。fire-and-forget；演出由同事 handler 渲染。\\n            const transferResult = this.m_gameView.RoundInfo?.TransferResult ?? [];\\n            if ( transferResult.length > 0 )\\n            {\\n                await this.PlaySymbolTransferEffect( transferResult );\\n            }\\n\\n            // 退場前停留一拍（讓玩家看清標記後的盤面）\\n            await Define.Wait( this.m_gameView, FeatureWheelShowState.STAY_DURATION );\\n\\n            // 衝突2(B2)：盤面縮小(RESTORE_ZOOM) 與 退場 Out 並行——盤面回正的同時轉輪退場，\\n            // 與進場「放大(ZoomIn)+Active 同時」對稱。兩者作用對象不同（GameView transform vs BigWheel Spine），\\n            // 無共用 target，可安全並行。\\n            await Promise.all( [ this.StopBoardEffect(), this.PlayWheelExit() ] );\\n        }\\n        finally\\n        {\\n            // 安全網：例外路徑保證盤面還原（happy path 已並行還原）。\\n            // RestoreZoom 冪等——m_isZoomed 在還原完成才設 false，故 happy path resolve 後此處 no-op，不會縮兩次。\\n            await this.StopBoardEffect();\\n        }\\n\\n        eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.RESUME_IDLE );\\n\\n        stateManager.NextState( Game_Define.GameState.ROUND_SHOW_END );\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 啟動進場盤面效果：單次全畫面震動（SHAKE，播完自停）+ 盤面往下（ZoomIn）。fire-and-forget，與 Active 同時。\\n     * 震動單次自停、不需配對；ZoomIn 記原始狀態，須與 StopBoardEffect 的 RESTORE 配對（由 OnEnter try/finally 保證）。\\n     */\\n    private StartBoardEffect()\\n    {\\n        eventManager.Dispatch( SHAKE_EVENT.SHAKE_WITH_NAME, \\\"feature_wheel_active\\\" );\\n        eventManager.Dispatch(\\n            REEL_MANAGER_EVENT.ZOOM_IN_REEL,\\n            FeatureWheelShowState.BOARD_ZOOM_POS,\\n            FeatureWheelShowState.BOARD_ZOOM_SCALE,\\n            FeatureWheelShowState.BOARD_ZOOM_IN_DURATION\\n        );\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 停止進場盤面效果：還原 ZoomIn；await 還原動畫播畢才 resolve（盤面回正後才續流程）。\\n     * 另發 SHAKE STOP 作安全網（單次震動通常已自停，此處確保退場不殘留震動）。\\n     * RESTORE_ZOOM 若未曾 ZoomIn（m_isZoomed=false）會立即回調，不會 hang。\\n     */\\n    private StopBoardEffect(): Promise<void>\\n    {\\n        eventManager.Dispatch( SHAKE_EVENT.STOP );\\n        return new Promise<void>( resolve =>\\n        {\\n            eventManager.Dispatch(\\n                REEL_MANAGER_EVENT.RESTORE_ZOOM,\\n                FeatureWheelShowState.BOARD_ZOOM_OUT_DURATION,\\n                () => resolve()\\n            );\\n        } );\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 包一層 Promise 等進場 Active 播畢（PLAY_ENTER；Spine 缺失由 Manager 立即回調）\\n     */\\n    private PlayWheelEnter(): Promise<void>\\n    {\\n        return new Promise<void>( resolve =>\\n        {\\n            eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.PLAY_ENTER, () => resolve() );\\n        } );\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 包一層 Promise 等退場 Out 播畢（PLAY_EXIT；Spine 缺失由 Manager 立即回調）\\n     */\\n    private PlayWheelExit(): Promise<void>\\n    {\\n        return new Promise<void>( resolve =>\\n        {\\n            eventManager.Dispatch( FEATURE_WHEEL_EVENT.PLAY_EXIT, () => resolve() );\\n        } );\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 包一層 Promise 等單次 PLAY_SPIN 完成回調\\n     * @param landIndex 落點格索引（已由呼叫端確認 0~11 );\\n    }\\n\\n    public OnProcess()\\n    {\\n\\n    }\\n\\n    public OnLeave()\\n    {\\n\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 播放飛行效果（FeatureWheel Trail：自輪盤中心飛向盤面落點，依功能 type 切 Skin）\\n     * 左/右 Trail 由「終點落在盤面哪一 column（0~5）」決定（col < 3 左、>= 3 右），逐落點各自判定。\\n     * @param data 飛行效果資料\\n     */\\n    private async PlayFlyEffect( data: ar2es2Proto.IFeatureResult )\\n    {\\n        log( \\\"PlayFlyEffect data: \\\" + JSON.stringify( data ) );\\n\\n        const positions = data.Positions ?? [];\\n        if ( positions.length === 0 )\\n        {\\n            return; // 無落點位置 → 無飛行可播\\n        }\\n\\n        // 起點：向 FeatureWheelManager 取目標節點（同步 dispatch；callback 立即回傳節點）\\n        let
